---
title: "Water Treatment and Cooling"
---

## Role in the Value Chain

Water treatment and cooling are part of the electrolyser balance of plant. They are separated here because they can influence auxiliary power, platform footprint, water logistics, mass, and reliability.

## Cost Model

Current cost is bundled in the non-electrical BOP cost:

| Parent component | Cost basis |
|---|---:|
| Non-electrical BOP | 380 EUR/kW |

No separate water-treatment or cooling CAPEX curve is currently documented.

## Energy Balance

Water treatment, cooling, gas handling, and purification are included in the BOP auxiliary load curve. The source material describes BOP losses as load-dependent and approximately 2% at full-load operation when purification losses are included.

## Availability

Availability is currently bundled into electrolyser availability.

## Future Refinement

If the model begins comparing centralised and decentralised seawater treatment layouts, this page should split:

- desalination and purification CAPEX
- cooling-system CAPEX
- auxiliary power by subsystem
- water consumption per kg H2
- platform or turbine mass implications
